---
title: "Academics"
---

<!-- Google Fonts -->
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500&family=Playfair+Display:wght@500;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">

<style>
body {
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  color: #333;
}

.hero a {
  color: #b45373;
  text-decoration: none;
  font-weight: 500;
}

.hero a:hover {
  text-decoration: underline;
}

.content a {
  color: #b45373;
  text-decoration: none;
}

.content a:hover {
  text-decoration: underline;
}

h1 {
  font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
  font-weight: 600;
  letter-spacing: 0.3px;
}

h3 {
  font-weight: 500;
}

.content {
  line-height: 1.7;
}
</style>

<!-- HERO -->
<div class="hero" style="
  text-align: center;
  padding: 64px 20px 48px;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, #fdecef 0%, #ffffff 100%);
  border-bottom: 1px solid #f2cbd4;
  margin-bottom: 48px;
">

For a complete record of my academic appointments, publications, teaching, and service, please see my  
**[curriculum vitae](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CV-Roberts-Jan2026.pdf)**.

</div>

<!-- MAIN CONTENT -->
<div class="content" style="
  max-width: 780px;
  margin: 0 auto 48px;
  font-size: 1.1em;
">

## Education

### PhD in Biostatistics, University of Michigan, 2016–2022

Dissertation: *Causal Inference Methods and Intermediate Endpoints in Randomized Clinical Trials*  

Advisors: Jeremy Taylor and Michael Elliott

### MS in Biostatistics, University of Michigan, 2018

### Bachelor of Arts in Mathematics and Psychology, Coe College, 2016

Summa Cum Laude  
GPA 3.99  

Mathematics Departmental Distinction  
Scholar’s Program Honors Thesis: *Roommate Matching by Design*  
Advisor: Jonathan White

## Selected Awards and Distinctions

Delta Omega Honorary Society in Public Health  
University of Michigan School of Public Health Regents’ Fellowship, 2016  
National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program, 2016–2021  
NIH Cancer Training Grant in Biostatistics, 2016–2017  

University of Michigan Rackham Predoctoral Fellowship, 2021–2022  
University of Michigan Department of Biostatistics Excellence in Research Award, Honorable Mention, 2021  

Michigan Institute of Data Science Symposium, 2019  
“Most Likely to Make an Impact in the Field” Award  

Michigan Student Symposium for Interdisciplinary Statistical Sciences, 2020  
Best Speed Oral Presentation Award  

Michigan Student Symposium for Interdisciplinary Statistics, 2021  
Best Speed Oral Presentation Award  

Society for Clinical Trials, 2022  
Best Poster Award  

American Statistical Association Lifetime Data Science Paper Award, 2023  

Junior Faculty Opportunity Award, University of Iowa, 2023  
New Faculty Research Award, University of Iowa, 2023  

American Statistical Association Outstanding Chapter Service Award, 2024  

College of Public Health Faculty Teaching Award, University of Iowa, 2025  

## Selected Grants and Awarded Funding

**NINDS NeuroNEXT**  
Coffey, C. (PI) · 09/2022–Present  
Role: Co‑Investigator  

**Pilot Grant, College of Public Health New Faculty Research Award**  
Role: Principal Investigator  
Project: Evaluating surrogate markers in a diabetes clinical trial using longitudinal and causal inference methods  

**Causal Inference Collaboratory Pilot Funding**  
IPHPRP, University of Iowa · 08/2024–07/2025  
Role: Principal Investigator  

Additional travel and career development awards include support from WSDS, Rackham Graduate School, SAMSI, the Telomere Network, JSM, LiDS Section, and the Carol S. Gleich Travel Award.
